articles.title: IMD forecasts scorching heat conditions to continue over parts of Northwest and Central India - News On AIR
articles.full_text:
The India Meteorological Department (IMD) has forecast scorching heat conditions to continue over parts of Northwest and Central India for the next three days and abate thereafter. According to IMD, Haryana, Chandigarh, Delhi, Himachal Pradesh, Jammu and Kashmir, Madhya Pradesh, Punjab, Rajasthan, Uttar Pradesh, Bihar, Chhattisgarh, Gujarat and Vidarbha will experience heatwave conditions till tomorrow.
The weather agency has predicted Hot and humid weather conditions in isolated pockets over Andhra Pradesh, Kerala, Mahe and Tamil Nadu today. It also said that Haryana, Chandigarh, Delhi, Chhattisgarh and West Madhya Pradesh will likely experience warm night conditions today. The met department has predicted heavy to very heavy rainfall at isolated places over Arunachal Pradesh, Assam and Meghalaya. The weather body predicted Heavy Rainfall at isolated places over Nagaland, Manipur, Mizoram, Tripura, West Bengal and Sikkim.

articles.title: IMD forecasts heatwave over Delhi, Punjab, Haryana, UP, West Rajasthan and adjoining regions - News On AIR
articles.full_text:
India Meteorological Department (IMD) has forecast heatwave conditions today over Delhi, Haryana, Chandigarh, Punjab, Himachal Pradesh, Jammu and Kashmir, Ladakh, Uttar Pradesh and West Rajasthan. Hot and Humid weather conditions are expected during the day at places over Konkan and Goa and Odisha.
Meanwhile, heavy rainfall conditions are expected over Arunachal Pradesh, Assam, Meghalaya, Coastal Karnataka and Sub Himalayan West Bengal, Sikkim. Parts of Bihar, Interior Karnataka, Kerala, Mahe, Konkan, Goa, Tamil Nadu Puducherry, Karaikal, Telangana and north-east India are also likely to witness rainfall during the next 2-3 days.
According to the Met Department thunder squalls are expected today over Bihar and Gangetic West Bengal. Thunderstorms and lightning are likely today in places over Andaman and Nicobar Islands, Andhra Pradesh, Chhattisgarh, Jharkhand, Madhya Pradesh, Maharashtra, Odisha, Sub Himalayan West Bengal, Sikkim, Tamil Nadu, Uttar Pradesh and Puducherry.
